```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(DEoptim)
library(lmomco)
library(DEoptim)
library(tidyr)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

# Input and Output Directories
metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir    <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Create the Plots directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD REGIONAL MASTER DATA
# =====================================================================
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))

cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
```
```{r}
library(DEoptim)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Define the Ratio of Power Laws Function
# f(k) = c + W * [ (k + d1)^-a / (k + d2)^-b ]
ratio_power_model <- function(k, alpha, beta, d1, d2, W, cc) {
  num <- (k + d1)^-alpha
  den <- (k + d2)^-beta
  return(cc + W * (num / den))
}

# 2. Filter data for k >= 24
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are already aligned in your environment
df_tail <- data.frame(k = k_seq, t2 = t2_val) %>%
  filter(k >= 24)

k_train <- df_tail$k
t2_train <- df_tail$t2

# 3. Objective Function
obj_func_tail <- function(params) {
  a   <- params[1]
  b   <- params[2]
  d1  <- params[3]
  d2  <- params[4]
  W   <- params[5]
  cc  <- params[6]
  
  preds <- ratio_power_model(k_train, a, b, d1, d2, W, cc)
  
  # Minimize SSE
  return(sum((t2_train - preds)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# 4. Optimization for the Tail
# We narrow the search for 'c' around your tail values (0.4 - 0.5)
set.seed(123)
fit_tail <- DEoptim(fn = obj_func_tail, 
                    lower = c(0.1, 0.1, 0.0, 0.0, 0.01, 0.35), 
                    upper = c(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 0.50), 
                    control = DEoptim.control(itermax = 300, trace = 50))

# 5. Extraction
res_t <- fit_tail$optim$bestmem
a_t <- res_t[1]; b_t <- res_t[2]
d1_t <- res_t[3]; d2_t <- res_t[4]
W_t <- res_t[5]; c_t <- res_t[6]

# 6. Visualization
k_smooth <- seq(24, 240, length.out = 200)
df_line <- data.frame(k = k_smooth) %>%
  mutate(y = ratio_power_model(k, a_t, b_t, d1_t, d2_t, W_t, c_t))

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = df_tail, aes(x = k, y = t2), size = 3, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = df_line, aes(x = k, y = y), color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Tail Fit (k >= 24): Ratio of Power Laws",
       subtitle = paste("Alpha:", round(a_t, 2), "| Beta:", round(b_t, 2), "| Limit C:", round(c_t, 4)),
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)") 
```

```{r}
# 1. Use the BEST parameters from your previous fit_tail optimization
# (Ensure these variables are in your environment from the previous step)
a_opt  <- res_t[1]
b_opt  <- res_t[2]
d1_opt <- res_t[3]
d2_opt <- res_t[4]
W_opt  <- res_t[5]
c_opt  <- res_t[6]

# 2. Generate predictions for ALL k (1 to 240)
k_all_seq <- seq(1, 240, length.out = 500)
df_validation <- data.frame(k = k_all_seq) %>%
  mutate(predicted = ratio_power_model(k, a_opt, b_opt, d1_opt, d2_opt, W_opt, c_opt))

# 3. Create a combined data frame for the original points
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are your original full vectors
df_original <- data.frame(k = k_seq, observed = t2_val) %>%
  mutate(region = ifelse(k >= 24, "Training (Tail)", "Validation (Head)"))

# 4. Plot the results
ggplot() +
  # The Full Prediction Line
  geom_line(data = df_validation, aes(x = k, y = predicted), 
            color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  # The Data Points (Colored by region)
  geom_point(data = df_original, aes(x = k, y = observed, color = region), size = 3) +
  # Vertical line at the training threshold
  geom_vline(xintercept = 24, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Training (Tail)" = "black", "Validation (Head)" = "orange")) +
  labs(title = "Model Validation: Tail-Optimized Parameters on All Data",
       subtitle = "Does the tail-fit predict the low-k hump?",
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)",
       color = "Data Region") +
  theme_minimal()
```




```{r}
# 1. Load Necessary Libraries
library(DEoptim)
library(VGAM)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Define the Gamma-Type Model
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# f(k) = cc + W * (k^gamma) * exp(-lambda * k)
gamma_model <- function(k, gamma, lambda, W, cc) {
  return(cc + W * (k^gamma) * exp(-lambda * k))
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Data Preparation
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are already defined in your environment.
# We bundle them to ensure 1-to-1 alignment.
full_df <- data.frame(k = k_seq, observed = t2_val) %>%
  mutate(region = ifelse(k >= 24, "Training (Tail)", "Validation (Head)"))

# Separate training data for the optimizer
train_df <- full_df %>% filter(region == "Training (Tail)")
k_train <- train_df$k
t2_train <- train_df$observed

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Objective Function with Peak POSITION and HEIGHT constraints
# ---------------------------------------------------------
obj_func_refined <- function(params) {
  g  <- params[1] # Growth exponent (gamma)
  l  <- params[2] # Decay rate (lambda)
  W  <- params[3] # Scaling weight
  cc <- params[4] # Asymptotic floor
  
  # Predict tail values (The Training Region)
  preds <- gamma_model(k_train, g, l, W, cc)
  
  # SSE for the tail
  # We multiply by 10 to make the tail fit higher priority than the penalties
  sse_tail <- 10 * sum((t2_train - preds)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # --- CONSTRAINTS FOR VALIDATION (The Hump) ---
  # Peak Position: Peak of k^g * exp(-l*k) is at k = g/l
  theoretical_peak_k <- g / l
  peak_pos_penalty <- 100 * (theoretical_peak_k - 5)^2
  
  # Peak Height: Target Value based on your orange points (~0.62)
  peak_height <- gamma_model(theoretical_peak_k, g, l, W, cc)
  peak_height_penalty <- 1000 * (peak_height - 0.623)^2
  
  # Return total loss to be minimized
  return(sse_tail + peak_pos_penalty + peak_height_penalty)
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Run the Optimization
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Bounds: alpha [0.01, 3], lambda [0.001, 1], W [0.001, 2], cc [0.1, 0.6]
lower_bounds <- c(0.01, 0.0001, 0.001, 0.10)
upper_bounds <- c(3.0,  1.0,    2.0,   0.60)

set.seed(42)
fit_final <- DEoptim(fn = obj_func_refined, 
                      lower = lower_bounds, 
                      upper = upper_bounds, 
                      control = DEoptim.control(itermax = 1000, trace = 100))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Extraction & Full Visualization
# ---------------------------------------------------------
res <- fit_final$optim$bestmem
g_opt <- res[1]; l_opt <- res[2]; W_opt <- res[3]; cc_opt <- res[4]

# Generate a smooth prediction line across the whole range
k_all_seq <- seq(0.1, max(k_seq), length.out = 600)
df_line <- data.frame(k = k_all_seq) %>%
  mutate(predicted = gamma_model(k, g_opt, l_opt, W_opt, cc_opt))

# Calculate where the peak actually ended up
final_peak_k <- g_opt / l_opt
final_peak_y <- gamma_model(final_peak_k, g_opt, l_opt, W_opt, cc_opt)

ggplot() +
  # The optimized model curve
  geom_line(data = df_line, aes(x = k, y = predicted), color = "darkblue", linewidth = 1.2) +
  # Original data points
  geom_point(data = full_df, aes(x = k, y = observed, color = region), size = 3) +
  # Annotate the training boundary
  geom_vline(xintercept = 24, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.35, 0.7)) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Training (Tail)" = "#F8766D", "Validation (Head)" = "#00BFC4")) +
  labs(title = "Refined Double-Constrained Gamma Model",
       subtitle = paste("Peak K:", round(final_peak_k, 2), 
                        "| Peak Height:", round(final_peak_y, 3), 
                        "| Floor C:", round(cc_opt, 3)),
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)") +
  theme_minimal()
```
